key_points: Minerals occur in different forms such as veins, lodes and placer deposits
Ferrous minerals contain iron and are important for metallurgical industries
Non-ferrous minerals such as copper and bauxite are widely used in electrical and engineering industries
Coal is the most important conventional source of energy in India
Renewable energy sources help reduce environmental pollution
examples: Magnetite and hematite are important types of iron ore
Bauxite is used to produce aluminium
Natural gas is used in fertilizer industries and power generation
Wind farms are developed in Tamil Nadu and Gujarat
formulas: Non-renewable resources = Resources that take millions of years to form
Hydroelectric power = Electricity generated from flowing water
exam_tips: Remember the characteristics of magnetite and hematite
Learn locations of major coalfields like Jharia and Raniganj
Understand advantages of natural gas as a fuel
Practice map-based questions on mineral distribution
